items.title: Global earthquake watch
items.summary: USGS reported 341 earthquake events in the last 24 hours. Strongest reported event: M5.4 near 145 km E of Hihifo, Tonga.
items.whyItMatters: Earthquake monitoring is a naturally recurring live-information topic with strong revisit potential and authoritative public data.

items.title: NASA live programming
items.summary: A recurring watch destination for NASA coverage, mission updates, and public live programming.
items.whyItMatters: A stable source-backed page can anchor repeat traffic around launches, mission milestones, and agency updates.

items.title: Artemis II
items.summary: Artemis II is on NASA's published launch schedule and is a strong candidate for a source-backed launch event page.
items.whyItMatters: Launches convert the vague launch placeholder into a real, source-backed event record. Topic hint: artemis.

items.title: Aurora watch
items.summary: Current geomagnetic conditions are near Kp 2. Recent peak reached Kp 3. NOAA SWPC monitoring remains active.
items.whyItMatters: Aurora and geomagnetic conditions drive recurring public-interest checking behavior and diversify the space category beyond launches.
